Keywords: hero above fold headline product image first viewport h1 cta
Layout Pattern: Split value proposition with product proof or full-bleed product/state image
Use When: The page must establish context and next action quickly
Avoid When: The image is decorative or hides the product
Copy Inputs: Audience; trigger problem; promise; primary CTA; secondary CTA
Visual Assets: Product screenshot, app state, service photo, or real outcome image
Interaction Notes: Primary CTA first, secondary CTA lower-emphasis; keep nav predictable
Accessibility Checks: One H1; CTA focus visible; image has alt text; no text/image overlap at 375px

Keywords: page hero feature pricing use case docs section intro
Layout Pattern: Compact hero with breadcrumb or page label, specific H1, supporting proof line
Use When: Internal pages need orientation before details
Avoid When: Using homepage-scale type for every internal page
Copy Inputs: Page family; specific job; proof signal; primary CTA
Visual Assets: Relevant product state or icon set
Interaction Notes: Anchor links optional for long pages
Accessibility Checks: H1 unique; breadcrumb readable; CTA target clear

Keywords: problem pain before state challenge user job motivation
Layout Pattern: Two-column problem/impact or narrative block with concrete before-state
Use When: A page needs to show why the capability matters
Avoid When: The problem is generic or fear-based without product relevance
Copy Inputs: User pain; current workaround; cost of inaction
Visual Assets: Before screenshot, diagram, or concise statistic
Interaction Notes: Use progressive disclosure for detailed pains
Accessibility Checks: Do not invent stats; headings preserve logical order

Keywords: proof outcomes metrics logos reviews credibility quantified benefits
Layout Pattern: Logo/metric strip with short interpretation text
Use When: Visitors need confidence before deeper evaluation
Avoid When: Metrics are unsourced or visually overpower the core message
Copy Inputs: Metric; source; customer segment; claim caveat
Visual Assets: Customer logos, verified metric cards, testimonial snippets
Interaction Notes: Tooltips can explain metric definitions
Accessibility Checks: Claims marked placeholder if not sourced; contrast passes

Keywords: feature grid capability cards benefits modules product features
Layout Pattern: Responsive card grid grouped by user job or workflow stage
Use When: Multiple capabilities must be scanned quickly
Avoid When: Cards repeat generic copy or use equal visual weight for unequal importance
Copy Inputs: Feature name; user outcome; proof; link target
Visual Assets: Icons, mini screenshots, or workflow thumbnails
Interaction Notes: Cards link to feature detail pages; avoid hover-only reveals
Accessibility Checks: Cards have stable height; keyboard focus enters links; icons labelled or decorative

Keywords: how it works process steps workflow setup onboarding
Layout Pattern: Numbered process or workflow diagram with input/output states
Use When: Users need confidence in the path from start to result
Avoid When: Steps are internal implementation details only
Copy Inputs: Start state; steps; result; time/effort
Visual Assets: Screenshots, step illustrations, flow diagram
Interaction Notes: Keep 3-5 steps; let secondary detail expand
Accessibility Checks: Step order exposed to screen readers; diagram has text equivalent

Keywords: workflow steps use case journey sequence scenario
Layout Pattern: Scenario timeline with trigger, action, system response, and result
Use When: Use-case or feature page must show concrete usage
Avoid When: The workflow is too abstract to build from
Copy Inputs: Persona; trigger; action; system response; result
Visual Assets: Annotated screenshots or simple flow panels
Interaction Notes: Use anchors for long workflows
Accessibility Checks: Each step has descriptive heading; annotations don't rely on color alone

Keywords: testimonial reviews customers quote story social proof
Layout Pattern: Quote cards or story previews tied to audience and result
Use When: Emotional trust or category credibility is needed
Avoid When: Quotes, logos, or metrics are invented
Copy Inputs: Quote; role; company/context; result
Visual Assets: Customer photo/logo if approved
Interaction Notes: Link to full customer story when possible
Accessibility Checks: Quotes are real or marked placeholder; cards do not overflow mobile

Keywords: trust security privacy compliance reliability safety
Layout Pattern: Grouped trust pillars with links to policy or details
Use When: Conversion involves risk, money, health, privacy, or marketplace safety
Avoid When: Trust is buried below conversion or uses vague badges
Copy Inputs: Policies; certifications; support promise; privacy stance
Visual Assets: Certification badges, security diagram, policy links
Interaction Notes: Use accordions for policy detail
Accessibility Checks: No absolute claims without proof; links have accessible names

Keywords: trust safety marketplace moderation verification cancellation privacy wellness risk
Layout Pattern: Policy pillar grid plus process explanation
Use When: Marketplaces, wellness, healthcare, finance, or data products need reassurance
Avoid When: Claims imply guaranteed safety or medical outcomes
Copy Inputs: Verification steps; support process; privacy limits; escalation path
Visual Assets: Policy screenshots, moderation flow, support state
Interaction Notes: Let users drill into details without blocking task
Accessibility Checks: Sensitive claims caveated; emergency/medical disclaimers when applicable

Keywords: pricing table plans tiers subscription package compare
Layout Pattern: Plan cards plus comparison table for important limits
Use When: Users need to choose between options
Avoid When: Plan limits are hidden or only visible in FAQ
Copy Inputs: Plan names; prices; included features; limits; trial terms
Visual Assets: Plan cards, billing toggle, trust/payment markers
Interaction Notes: Billing toggle must update all visible prices
Accessibility Checks: Table scrolls or stacks on mobile; CTA per plan has clear label

Keywords: comparison compare alternatives plan feature matrix
Layout Pattern: Compact matrix that exposes real tradeoffs
Use When: Plan or product alternatives need evaluation
Avoid When: Every row has the same value across plans
Copy Inputs: Capabilities; limits; best-fit user
Visual Assets: Matrix or comparison cards
Interaction Notes: Use sticky headers only if they do not obscure content
Accessibility Checks: Screen reader can map row/column labels; no tiny unreadable text

Keywords: faq questions objections accordion help support
Layout Pattern: Accordion or grouped Q&A by decision stage
Use When: Common objections can block conversion
Avoid When: FAQ hides critical price/security terms
Copy Inputs: Questions; answers; owner/source
Visual Assets: None required; optional support icon
Interaction Notes: Accordions should be keyboard-operable
Accessibility Checks: Expanded state announced; content remains indexable when needed

Keywords: comparison competitors alternatives before after feature value
Layout Pattern: Side-by-side cards or before/after sequence
Use When: Users compare approaches or need category education
Avoid When: Competitors are attacked without evidence
Copy Inputs: Alternative; tradeoff; evidence; decision rule
Visual Assets: Before/after screenshots, comparison matrix
Interaction Notes: Keep language factual and sourced
Accessibility Checks: No false competitor claims; mobile stacks preserve labels

Keywords: app preview screenshot product demo interface mobile desktop state
Layout Pattern: Device/browser frame or unframed product screenshot with callouts
Use When: The product state is more persuasive than abstract claims
Avoid When: The visual is stock-like or too cropped to inspect
Copy Inputs: Screen state; callout labels; key action
Visual Assets: Screenshots, demo video, interactive preview
Interaction Notes: Use static fallback for video/animation
Accessibility Checks: Alt text explains state; callouts readable on mobile

Keywords: content hub resources blog guides articles cards filters
Layout Pattern: Searchable or filterable resource grid with topic clusters
Use When: SEO/resource pages need depth and findability
Avoid When: Everything is one chronological list
Copy Inputs: Topic clusters; featured guides; article metadata
Visual Assets: Article cards, author photos if real
Interaction Notes: Filters should have empty states and deep links
Accessibility Checks: Cards have descriptive titles; dates/authors accurate

Keywords: quickstart install setup docs developer getting started
Layout Pattern: Step-by-step install path with copyable code
Use When: Developer needs fastest path to first success
Avoid When: Marketing copy delays setup
Copy Inputs: Prerequisites; commands; expected output; troubleshooting
Visual Assets: Code snippets, terminal output, architecture diagram
Interaction Notes: Copy buttons optional but useful
Accessibility Checks: Code wraps safely; language labels present; prerequisites visible

Keywords: nav index sidebar docs table of contents links
Layout Pattern: Sidebar or in-page index organized by task
Use When: Long docs/resources need scan and return paths
Avoid When: Navigation mirrors internal org instead of user tasks
Copy Inputs: Sections; task groups; active state
Visual Assets: None required
Interaction Notes: Sticky nav only when viewport supports it
Accessibility Checks: Current page exposed; keyboard skip path exists

Keywords: cta final conversion band next step closing action
Layout Pattern: Full-width closing band with one primary action and one confidence note
Use When: Page needs a clear final next step
Avoid When: Multiple competing CTAs dilute action
Copy Inputs: Primary action; supporting note; risk reducer
Visual Assets: Optional product image or icon
Interaction Notes: Repeat primary CTA after proof, not before every block
Accessibility Checks: CTA label is specific; focus and contrast pass

Keywords: mission values company about credibility principles
Layout Pattern: Narrative plus proof cards tying mission to behavior
Use When: About page needs substance beyond slogans
Avoid When: Values are generic and unsupported
Copy Inputs: Mission; principles; product behaviors; proof
Visual Assets: Team photo, timeline, product principles
Interaction Notes: Keep scanning rhythm with short sections
Accessibility Checks: Photos have alt; no decorative wall of text

Keywords: contact options support sales demo booking channels
Layout Pattern: Channel cards plus short routing guidance
Use When: Users may need sales, support, or partnership paths
Avoid When: One generic form handles all intents poorly
Copy Inputs: Intent types; response times; channel owners
Visual Assets: Calendar, contact cards, map if local
Interaction Notes: Route by intent before showing detailed forms
Accessibility Checks: Forms labelled; response expectations visible

Keywords: lead form contact form demo booking quote signup
Layout Pattern: Short form grouped by routing need and privacy note
Use When: High-intent users need contact or demo
Avoid When: Asking for unnecessary fields upfront
Copy Inputs: Required fields; routing fields; consent/privacy copy
Visual Assets: None required
Interaction Notes: Inline validation and keyboard-specific inputs
Accessibility Checks: Labels visible; errors near fields; privacy note linked
